items.reason: All 20 decisions DEC-001 through DEC-020 listed in `docs/decisions/decisions-needed.md` exist in `docs/decisions/standards-overlap-decisions.md` with `id`, `question`, `options_considered`, `choice`, `consequences`, and `projects_to`. The required platform-shape decisions (DEC-011 privacy-surfaces, DEC-014 static-mirror-policy, DEC-015 service-role-policy, DEC-016 dictionary-generation-direction, DEC-017 canonical-field-reconciliation, DEC-018 global-enum-crosswalks, DEC-019 closed-privacy-classes, DEC-020 relational-graph-migrations) are all present with non-TBD consequences and projects_to lists.

`docs/decisions/decisions-pending.md` lists PEND-001 (owner Andy, target 2026-05-15, blocker product priority) and PEND-005 (owner Andy, target 2026-05-03, blocker CI capacity), each with rationale. Both target dates are in the future relative to the loop's research date 2026-04-27. No bare TBDs found.

Each decision's `consequences` opens with concrete-deletion bullets, e.g. DEC-001 `Removes per-function subject-to-person merge branches from supabase/functions/lti-launch-handler/index.ts`, DEC-007 `Removes duplicate source-ID columns from every public table`, DEC-016 `Removes direct edits to dictionary/oneroster-core.v1.json...`. Each names specific artifacts or code paths the decision deletes or collapses, and includes 'Why not collapse' justification when preserving multiple surfaces.

Decisions list three credible architectural options. DEC-005 considers LTI-AGS-as-canonical (a real production choice some LMSs make), peer source-native stores, and the chosen state/event/command separation. DEC-009 considers QTI-as-canonical-content vs source-native catalogs vs broad-resource concept. DEC-016 considers commit-only-shared, commit-only-per-spec, and the chosen upstream-shared/generated-projection split. None are obvious strawmen.

`scripts/generate_spec_dictionaries.py` reads `data/data-dictionary.seed.json` and writes the five `dictionary/*.v1.json` projections. VERIFY.md runs `python3 scripts/generate_spec_dictionaries.py --check` and `scripts/check_dictionary_artifacts.py` invokes the same `--check` for drift. The seed projects 575 fields across 59 objects across 5 specs (per `projection_summary`), enough to cover all per-spec fields.

`canonical.identity.person.email` in the seed lists projections to `dictionary/oneroster-core.v1.json#person.email`, `dictionary/caliper-core.v1.json#caliper_actor.email`, `dictionary/integration-governance-core.v1.json#lti_membership.email`, and `dictionary/qti-core.v1.json#qti_candidate.email`. Identity, organization, course, class, role, membership, line-item and result canonical fields each enumerate cross-spec projection lists. The data resolves the overlaps, not prose.

`data/data-dictionary.seed.json` declares `canonical_objects[].canonical.identity.person` with its own fields (id, display_name, email, source_subject_id, identity_kind), allowed-value enum, and a projections list mapping OneRoster `person`, Caliper `caliper_actor`, QTI `qti_candidate`, and LTI `lti_membership`/`lti_launch`. The unification is data, including a documented `spec_only` exception for QTI candidate.

Per-spec dictionaries reference `shared_enum_id` (e.g. `oneroster-core.v1.json` `result.score_status` -> `grading_progress`). The shared `grading_progress` enum lists OneRoster `fullyGraded`, `partiallyGraded`, `submitted`, `notSubmitted` and LTI `FullyGraded`, `Pending`, `PendingManual`, `Failed`, `NotReady` mapped bidirectionally to canonical values, with `reverse_crosswalk` blocks present. Same pattern visible for `record_status`, `identifier_type`, `organization_type`, etc.

DEC-019 fixes the closed list to nine concrete classes (`public`, `operational`, `directory`, `education_record`, `behavioral`, `sensitive`, `credential`, `privacy_governance`, `system`). `scripts/generate_spec_dictionaries.py` defines `INVALID_PRIVACY_CLASSES = {'depends_on_contents', 'depends_on_entity'}` and explicitly errors on those plus any `depends_on_*` placeholder, validating every object/field/spec_field/canonical-object record.

The seed's `migration_generation` block declares the runtime object keys, table order, and 13 relationship keys; runtime objects carry `relationships[]` per DEC-020. `scripts/generate_supabase_migrations.py` reads the graph and writes `supabase/migrations/0001_oneroster_core_demo.sql`. VERIFY.md runs `--check` to enforce zero drift, and `check_dictionary_artifacts.py` invokes it transitively.

`scripts/check_dictionary_artifacts.py` enforces `REQUIRED_FIELD_KEYS` containing `decision_id` on every field, validates the id resolves in the decision-trace block extracted from `docs/decisions/standards-overlap-decisions.md`, and verifies the field appears under that decision's `produces_fields`. The decision-trace JSON listed in the register covers DEC-001 through DEC-020.

`scripts/generate_spec_fidelity_report.py` exists and VERIFY.md runs both `--report-only` and `--check`, satisfying the seam-enforcement plumbing. However `docs/loop-overrides.md` (2026-04-27) records that the new generator detected 37 silent renames in OneRoster (`personId` -> spec `user`, `classId` -> `class`, etc.) and that all 138 integration-governance-core fields lacked `extension_rationale`. No evidence in this batch (e.g. a current `docs/spec-fidelity-report.md` showing zero defects, or extension_rationale strings in the truncated `dictionary/integration-governance-core.v1.json`) confirms those have been fully cleared. The substance_check fails on a single non-EXTENSION_SPEC_KEYS extension without rationale or 10+ EXTENSION_SPEC_KEYS fields lacking rationale.
items.traced_cause: data/data-dictionary.seed.json: align every 1EdTech-native spec_field so spec_field.json_name == spec_field.sourceStandard.field and spec_field.column_name == snake_case(sourceStandard.field); add extension_rationale on every field of integration-governance-core; regenerate so docs/spec-fidelity-report.md shows zero copy-exact seam defects and zero missing-rationale extensions.

items.reason: Each per-spec generator (`scripts/generate_oneroster_core.py`, `generate_qti_core.py`, `generate_case_core.py`, `generate_caliper_core.py`, `generate_integration_governance_core.py`) emits Markdown and HTML dictionary pages from the same JSON source. `scripts/check_dictionary_artifacts.py` verifies object headings, field column names, decision IDs, sourceStandard strings, and HTML descriptions are present in the rendered docs. `scripts/build_site_docs.py` renders the Markdown into the portal.

`docs/lead-spec-accounting.md` includes a Decision Citation Map mapping each Lead area to its decision id with a plain-language sentence (e.g. `DEC-005-results-scores`: 'Gradebook line items and results use a stable result contract...'). The buildability guide links DEC-010 inline (`per [DEC-010](decisions-standards-overlap-decisions.html#DEC-010-tenancy-reference-data)`). The decision register itself renders at `site/docs/decisions-standards-overlap-decisions.html` with per-decision anchors verified by `scripts/check_site_links.py`.

`docs/build-an-edtech-app.md` walks all five required steps with copy-pasteable curl against the live Supabase REST URL: (1) creates org + teacher + 10 students, (2) creates session/course/class with full enrollments, (3) posts a numeric grade via `line_items` and `results`, (4) PATCHes `line_items.case_target_uri` with a CASE URI, (5) inserts a Caliper `GradeEvent` and reads `class_activity_feed`. Each step prefaces a dictionary-link table with both field anchors (e.g. `oneroster-core-dictionary.html#person.email`) and global enum anchors (e.g. `enum.role_family.teacher`).

`site/api/site-link-check.json` reports `status: pass`, `errors: 0`, with 226 local links checked, 634 dictionary entries verified, 20 decision anchors checked, and 12 API endpoints checked. All 37 doc pages and 4 OpenAPI pages are reachable from `site/index.html` within the 3-click budget; the buildability guide reaches in 1 click.

Most steps are executable. Step 0 instructs new developers to either use the hosted sandbox (requires platform admin) or load the migration into their own Supabase project; the SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY path is documented. However Step 5's `caliper_events` insert sends `envelope_id` and `raw_event`, which appear in the `caliper_events` migration but are NOT listed in the step's dictionary-link table — the layperson would have to guess the field semantics. Also, Step 0 directs the reader to copy `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY` into a shell, but the safer pattern (admin function or Edge Function) is not offered, leaving privacy ambiguity for an unauthenticated layperson trying step 1.
items.traced_cause: docs/build-an-edtech-app.md (Step 5): expand the dictionary-link table to include caliper_events.envelope_id and caliper_events.raw_event with anchors to caliper-core-dictionary.html, since those columns appear in the curl payload. Optionally add an admin-operations note explaining service-role exposure to a layperson per DEC-015.

items.reason: This evaluator output emits `traced_cause` on every non-pass item naming the lowest-layer artifact (e.g. `data/data-dictionary.seed.json` field-name alignment for spec_fidelity_provable; `docs/build-an-edtech-app.md` Step 5 dictionary-link table for buildable_by_layperson) and the required change. `buildability_gaps` array names step, gap, and traced cause.

VERIFY.md ends with `python3 scripts/evaluate_platform.py --output site/api/platform-evaluation.json`, so each iteration writes a fresh report. `scripts/codex_loop.py` calls `run_evaluator(repo, args, label='before')` and `'after'` per iteration and reads `site/api/platform-evaluation.json` to drive termination and stall detection. Report schema includes per-item status and a buildability_gaps array.

`scripts/codex_loop.py` calls `read_overrides(repo)` at the start of every iteration before launching Codex, then `overrides_paused()` halts the loop before the next iteration when a literal `PAUSE` token sits on its own line under `## Pause` (HTML comments stripped). `build_prompt` injects the cleaned overrides under a 'Human overrides from `docs/loop-overrides.md` (AUTHORITATIVE — these supersede rubric defaults...)' section.

`scripts/codex_loop.py` re-reads `site/api/platform-evaluation.json` via `run_evaluator(..., label='after')` after VERIFY, and termination uses `if after_eval and after_eval.get('done'): return 0`. The `LOOP_STATUS: COMPLETE` branch explicitly logs 'Codex reported COMPLETE but evaluator says work remains. Continuing.' when the verify log says `done=False`. No numeric-threshold gating is used.

`maybe_publish` in `scripts/codex_loop.py` runs `git fetch origin main` then `git pull --rebase origin main` before `git push origin main`. On rebase failure it captures conflicting paths via `git diff --name-only --diff-filter=U`, runs `git rebase --abort`, and returns a `DIVERGENCE:` string. The main loop returns 1 immediately on a `DIVERGENCE:` publish_result, halting further iterations. PROGRESS.md gets the conflicting paths and the recommended `git fetch ... && git pull --rebase ... && git push` resolution command.

buildability_gaps.gap: Step 5's curl posts `envelope_id` and `raw_event` JSON keys to `caliper_events`, but the dictionary-link table at the top of Step 5 omits those two columns, so a layperson would have to infer them from the SQL migration to know they exist or what they mean.
buildability_gaps.traced_cause: docs/build-an-edtech-app.md Step 5 dictionary-link table: add rows for `caliper_events.envelope_id` and `caliper_events.raw_event` linking to their entries in `site/docs/caliper-core-dictionary.html`.

buildability_gaps.gap: Step 0 has the layperson export `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY` into the shell, but does not state plainly which privacy/audit boundary that crosses (DEC-011/DEC-015) or that this key must never appear in browser code. A layperson with general web knowledge could leak it.
buildability_gaps.traced_cause: docs/build-an-edtech-app.md Step 0: add a one-paragraph callout linking to DEC-015-service-role-policy and admin-operations.html explaining that the service-role key is only for the one-time admin user creation and must stay in a trusted shell.

projections.note: Structural per-decision projection index generated from `projects_to`; semantic contradictions are graded by the projections_match_reality rubric item.

decisions.title: DEC-001 Person, User, Actor, Profile, and Subject
decisions.choice: The platform has a canonical `person` for known school people and a broader `agent` concept for non-person actors. Credential subjects and Caliper actors resolve to `person` only when policy and identifiers allow it; otherwise they remain linked external agents.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.
Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-002 Class, Course, Context, Group, Course Section, and Organization
decisions.choice: The platform separates `course` from `class`. `course` is the catalog/template. `class` is the scheduled teaching section. LTI contexts and Caliper groups can map to a class, course, or other learning context. Common Cartridge organizations are content outlines, not school organizations or scheduled classes.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-003 Roles
decisions.choice: Store source roles exactly, then map them to platform role families: `learner`, `educator`, `administrator`, `guardian`, `mentor`, `staff`, `tool`, `issuer`, and `unknown`.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.
Referenced artifact contains the projection's primary token.

decisions.title: DEC-004 Enrollment and Membership
decisions.choice: `enrollment` is the canonical roster participation record. `membership` is a contextual service/event view that may be derived from enrollment or captured from an external system.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-005 Results, Scores, Outcomes, and Grade Events
decisions.choice: The platform uses `line_item` as the gradebook target and `result` as the learner's current gradebook state. LTI AGS Score is an incoming write/update message. LTI AGS Result and OneRoster Result map to current result state. QTI outcomes are assessment-runtime variables projected into results only when a delivery or scoring workflow declares that mapping. Caliper GradeEvent is event history, not the gradebook source of truth.

decisions.title: DEC-006 Standards Alignment
decisions.choice: CASE is the canonical standards graph. Other standards store alignment as source metadata and resolve known targets to CASE item identifiers or URIs.

decisions.title: DEC-007 Identifiers
decisions.choice: Internal records have platform IDs. Standard-shaped endpoints expose the standard-native ID as the primary contract when required. Every external ID is stored in a source identifier crosswalk with source system, identifier type, and object relationship.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-008 Time, School Sessions, and Event Timestamps
decisions.choice: Separate calendar periods, event timestamps, availability windows, and validity periods. Store timestamps in UTC with source timezone/offset when provided. Store OneRoster AcademicSession as the school calendar backbone.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-009 Content, Resources, Packages, and Launch Links
decisions.choice: The platform uses a broad `resource` concept for discoverable learning objects, with subtype-specific records for cartridge resources, QTI packages/items/tests, LTI resource links, and Caliper event entities.

decisions.title: DEC-010 Tenant-Owned Data and Shared Reference Data
decisions.choice: Tenant-owned operational records carry tenant boundaries and row-level policy. Public CASE frameworks, public standards metadata, public certification fixtures, and optionally public item banks live in shared reference namespaces. Tenants adopt, pin, override, or privately extend shared reference data through explicit records.

projections.detail: Referenced artifact contains the projection's primary token.
Referenced artifact contains the projection's primary token.

decisions.title: DEC-011 Privacy Surfaces
decisions.choice: Generated docs may describe every field and privacy class because they are schema documentation, not records. Live PostgREST may expose tenant-owned `public`, `operational`, `directory`, and `education_record` records only through tenant RLS and scope policy. Edge Functions may expose `directory`, `education_record`, `behavioral`, or sensitive operational fields only through caller JWT, purpose, scope, tenant RLS, and audit logging when the read is sensitive. Static `site/api/*.json` mirrors may contain only synthetic demo records from the seeded `.test` tenant and may include `public`, `operational`, and `directory` fields such as demo email; they may not contain real tenant data, `education_record`, `behavioral`, `sensitive_pii`, or secret/system values.

decisions.title: DEC-012 Runtime Coverage Per Spec
decisions.choice: OneRoster core is runtime-backed now through local SQLite, hosted Supabase PostgREST, RLS, and live tests. The teaching-app runtime also backs the narrow CASE alignment path needed by gradebook line items (`line_items.case_target_uri`) and the narrow Caliper class-feed path needed by the buildability guide (`caliper_events` plus `class_activity_feed`). QTI and full CASE import/search remain doc-only/generated dictionary projections until runtime slices are built. Caliper, LTI, Security Framework, and Data Privacy have partial runtime backing through authenticated table, view, Edge Function receipt, or governance paths, but not full standard conformance flows. Full runtime for QTI import, CASE search, full Sensor API ingestion/projection, LTI Advantage services, production OAuth, and privacy workflows remains future work with target dates in the decision/pending work queue.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.
Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-013 Audit Response Truth
decisions.choice: Audited Edge Functions must use truthful responses when they include an `audit` block. If the function cannot read the written `audit_log` rows back under the caller's JWT and request identifier, it must omit the audit block or return an explicit error; it must not hard-code `logged` counts or field lists.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.
Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-014 Static Mirror Policy
decisions.choice: Static mirrors are allowed as documentation fixtures for GitHub Pages and offline inspection. They are not the canonical runtime contract; live Supabase REST and Edge Functions are the runtime contract. Mirrors must be generated from the same synthetic demo seed as the local demo, must carry source metadata, and may only include privacy classes allowed by `DEC-011-privacy-surfaces`.

decisions.title: DEC-015 Service-Role Policy
decisions.choice: Request-scoped runtime code and Edge Functions must pass the caller's `Authorization` bearer token to Supabase so RLS applies. Service-role access is allowed only for allowlisted admin or test fixture operations in `docs/admin-operations.md`, with a named operation ID, caller, why-user-JWT-insufficient reason, guardrails, and recent review date. Tests may use service role to create temporary Auth users, but not to read tenant data or satisfy the assertion under test.

decisions.title: DEC-016 Dictionary Generation Direction
decisions.choice: `data/data-dictionary.seed.json` is upstream. `dictionary/*.v1.json` files are generated projections; hand-editing a per-spec projection is a build error once the generator lands.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.
Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-017 Canonical Field Reconciliation
decisions.choice: Every overlapping spec field carries `canonical_field_id` pointing at the shared dictionary. Spec-native names remain for conformance; cross-spec app code uses the canonical field identity.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-018 Global Enum Crosswalks
decisions.choice: Allowed values are global dictionary objects. Each enum field references `shared_enum_id`; the shared enum maps every spec-native value to and from a canonical value.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-019 Closed Privacy Classes
decisions.choice: Closed keys are `public`, `operational`, `directory`, `education_record`, `behavioral`, `sensitive`, `credential`, `privacy_governance`, and `system`. `depends_on_entity` and `depends_on_contents` are invalid.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-020 Relational Graph Migrations
decisions.choice: Shared dictionary objects own source field, target object, target field, cardinality, ownership, and delete behavior. Supabase migrations are generated from that graph.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.
Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.
